bullets: Conceptual essay on using language models in qualitative research; it describes the Skimle.com analysis tool but reports no empirical sample, geography, or study period.

Does not name a specific model; proposes three design criteria, predictable processes, end-to-end data-to-conclusion transparency, and researcher control over coding, implemented in the tool, with no validation reported.
Argues that method-agnostic tools built to these criteria augment rather than automate expert interpretation, countering hallucination and the cognitive offloading that erodes engagement with data.

Simulation benchmark, FinPersona-Bench, in which a synthetic market separates observable price from hidden fundamental value; 18 frontier and open-source LLMs each run under one of three behavioral mandates.
The 18 models, not individually named, act as autonomous trading agents; the benchmark measures mandate salience decay across three failure modes, with no comparison to human coding.
Mandate influence decays over long horizons and is model-dependent; in crashes the behavioral gap between static and periodically re-grounded agents grows 4.4 times by the final quarter.
